scenario.context: Station 7 of the daily run: protection needs for data exchange and mobile storage media.
scenario.mission: You assess secure alternatives, approval rules, and documentation duties for mobile data exchange.

station.profile: External data handover, approval processes, and encrypted transport

customerRequest.subject: Station 07: secure data exchange with mobile storage media
customerRequest.body: Please define a secure procedure for mobile data exchange. We need approval rules, secure alternatives, and a short documentation guideline.

questions.title: Best standard option for sensitive data
questions.prompt: Which option is usually the most sensible for sensitive data?

options.text: An approved encrypted transfer path or a managed storage-media process
A private unknown USB stick without approval
options.rationale: That is neither secure nor process-compliant.
That is not suitable for sensitive data.

feedback.correct: Correct: approved and protected paths are the best choice here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: sensitive data needs protection and approval.

questions.title: Rules for mobile data exchange
questions.prompt: Which rules make professional sense? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Follow the approval process before use
options.rationale: Use has to be clarified in advance.

options.text: Use suitable encryption and access protection
options.rationale: That lowers the risk in case of loss or misdelivery.

options.text: Prefer secure alternatives such as approved exchange services
options.rationale: Alternatives are often safer and easier to document.

feedback.correct: Correct: you connect approval, protection, and traceability.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: please prioritize approval, encryption, and secure alternatives more strongly.

questions.title: Secure process for media approval
items: Choose an approved storage medium or secure exchange path
Check deletion or return after completion
correctOrder: Choose an approved storage medium or secure exchange path
Check deletion or return after completion

feedback.correct: Correct: the sequence connects protection, proof, and completion control.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: first clarify necessity, then protection, documentation, and completion control.

questions.title: Documentation rate in the media process
questions.prompt: Out of 12 media transactions, 9 were fully documented. What is the documentation rate in percent?

feedback.wrong: Please divide the fully documented transactions by the total and convert the result to percent.

questions.title: Approval note for data exchange
questions.prompt: In 4-6 sentences, write an approval note with the exchange path, protection measure, and documentation duty.
questions.expectedOutline: Name the approved path, the protection measure, and the clear documentation duty.

feedback.correct: Correct: the note is secure, concise, and practical for daily work.
feedback.wrong: Please formulate the exchange path, the protection measure, and the documentation duty more clearly.
